name: Voucher canceled by member ID and voucher ID
description: Documented Sample JSON Response (lines 22704-22729) for a successful CancelVoucher process run. Returns the canceled VoucherId nested under outputParameters.outputParameters.results. Maps to the documented request variant using MemberId + VoucherId.
notes: Source: Loyalty Management Business APIs Developer Guide v67.0, "Cancel a Voucher" section (lines 22636-22733) plus the shared loyalty-program-process resource definition (lines 22540-22633). HTTP=POST, Available version=56.0, Requires Chatter=No. CAVEATS / org-configured: The guide explicitly states "The request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the CancelVoucher process type in a Salesforce org" (line 22673). So the exact field set inside each processParameters object and inside outputParameters.results is implementer-defined, docVerified=true reflects that I read the literal documented JSON examples and property tables, but the per-org field set should be treated as configurable. The standard request wrapper is processParameters (a Loyalty Process Parameter Map, Required, v56.0). The response is a "Loyalty Process Output" object; the doc presents it only as the inline JSON example (no separate Response Bodies property table for this object was found, grep for "Loyalty Process Output" returns only this one inline reference at line 22633). Documented response top-level keys: message (null in example), outputParameters (double-nested: outputParameters.outputParameters.results[]), simulationDetails (empty object {} in example; populated only in simulation mode), status (boolean). Field name interchangeability note (line 22678): some request fields have interchangeable names (e.g., MemberId / MembershipNumber, VoucherId / VoucherCode) per "Loyalty Management Connect API Interchangeable Request Fields". Voucher-code uniqueness caveat (line 22692): if a voucher code isn't unique, supply a voucher ID or number. No error/empty fixture created, the doc shows no error or failure response example for this process. Example URL uses program name "DemoProgram" and full path /services/data/v67.0/connect/loyalty/programs/DemoProgram/program-processes/CancelVoucher.
